**SLOT 2 — (incoming-call-dialog-not-rendered) Tier 1-2 — investigation, ~45-90 min**
After the 2026-05-23 PM smoke, alice clicking "Call Sales (online)" successfully creates a call (URL changes to /app/call/[id]) and opens the LiveKit signal connection. But bob's page (also at /app, signed in via bypass as bob@yelli.local, Sales dept binding) shows `[role="dialog"]` count = 0 — no IncomingCallDialog renders. The actual cause is one of: (a) IncomingCallDialog is not mounted on the /app route at all (only on some other layout subtree); (b) the socket emits `call:incoming` but bob's client-side listener isn't subscribed yet because of a fresh-client race (similar to but distinct from the resolved [[fresh-client-presence-snapshot-race]]); (c) the server-side `call:incoming` payload doesn't reach bob because of tenant/org/dept filtering in the socket server's emit logic. Investigation steps: (1) grep `IncomingCallDialog` to find where it's mounted in the component tree and confirm /app contains it; (2) Playwright instrument bob's page to capture all `framereceived` events on the Socket.IO WebSocket — verify whether `call:incoming` ever arrives at bob's client; (3) if it doesn't arrive: trace the server-side emit path in `apps/web/src/server/socket/server.ts` (or equivalent) for any filter that excludes bob; (4) if it arrives but dialog doesn't render: trace the React handler that's supposed to set dialog state. Use the same multi-context rig technique from [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-22]]. This is the LAST blocker before the smoke can be converted from PARTIAL → FULL per [[phase-7-realtime-engine-closeout-criterion]].

After SLOT 2 closes: re-run the full multi-context smoke. If both #14 (yellow-dot indicator confirmation) and #15 (IncomingCallDialog) pass, the smoke is FULL and Phase 7 realtime engines are closed at the strongest possible gate. Otherwise file a third follow-up. Also `git push origin main` to ship both SLOT 1 + SLOT 2 commits together (currently 1 commit ahead).
